title: Hippolytia aphanassievii
intro: Hippolytia aphanassievii, also known as Golden Ragwort, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the aster family (Asteraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Hippolytia aphanassievii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Hippolytia aphanassievii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of tinctures and ointments to treat various health conditions, particularly those related to the respiratory system.
taxonomy: Hippolytia aphanassievii is a plant species classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Hippolytia, which is a group of flowering plants. The classification of Hippolytia aphanassievii also includes the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida.
